_comment: The Execution Agent is the only agent on the payment path. It goes through the MCP service router, never raw endpoints. For a registered payer, Policy hands over the payer-bound capability from verify_payer_account; Execution first calls bind_execution_capability to re-issue it as an Execution-bound capability (the server-side Execution-Agent-only rule requires this), then create_payment_intent (no money moves) and submit_payment (after explicit payer confirmation). For a guest with no payer account, Execution uses its own biller capability directly — no verification or rebind — and the payment is recorded with no payer account. Identity (biller_id, payer_id) is bound to the capability token, never a tool argument.

function.name: bind_execution_capability
function.description: Re-issue the payer-bound capability handed over from Policy as an Execution-bound capability, so create_payment_intent/submit_payment pass the server-side Execution-Agent-only check for a registered payer. Backed by the MCP router tool bind_execution_capability. Preserves the biller, run, payer binding, and write scope; only the agent id is rebound. No money moves. Guest pay does not use this — a guest uses the biller capability directly.

capability_token.description: Write-capable payer-bound capability returned by verify_payer_account and handed over by Policy.

function.name: create_payment_intent
function.description: Quote a payment for the verified payer and return a confirmation-required intent (carrying an idempotency key) for the payer to approve. Backed by the MCP router tool create_payment_intent. Requires a write-capable, payer-bound capability. NO money moves here — present the returned total/fees to the payer and obtain explicit confirmation before calling submit_payment.

capability_token.description: Write-capable payer-bound capability returned by verify_payer_account.

invoice_id.description: The invoice to pay, from the payment plan.

method.description: Payment method from the plan; must be one of the biller's configured payment methods.

scheduled_for.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) to schedule the payment for, from the plan's 'when'; null to pay immediately.

function.name: submit_payment
function.description: Submit an approved payment intent, moving money via the idempotent Payment Service. Backed by the MCP router tool submit_payment. CALL ONLY after an explicit prior {confirm: true} from the client for the pending plan — approval from the Policy Agent is NOT confirmation. Pass the intent_id from create_payment_intent as the idempotency key; resubmitting the same intent resolves to the original payment (no double charge). Returns payment_id, confirmation code, amount_cents, fee_cents, and receipt_message.

capability_token.description: Write-capable payer-bound capability returned by verify_payer_account.

intent_id.description: The intent id returned by create_payment_intent; used as the idempotency key.

invoice_id.description: The invoice id from the approved intent.

method.description: The payment method from the approved intent.

payer_confirmed.description: Must be true: the payer has explicitly confirmed this payment. The router refuses to submit otherwise.

scheduled_for.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) from the approved intent; null to pay immediately.
